Description: Iraq's invasion of Kuwait disrupted oil supplies and triggered the Gulf War.
Coalition forces launched military operations to liberate Kuwait.
Financial crisis reduced oil demand across Asia, causing prices to decline.
U.S. and U.K. airstrikes against Iraq increased geopolitical uncertainty.
Terrorist attacks in the United States created uncertainty in global financial and energy markets.
U.S.-led invasion of Iraq raised concerns about oil supply disruptions.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global recession and reduced oil demand.
Political unrest across the Middle East and North Africa affected oil-producing countries.
Conflict in Libya disrupted oil production and exports.
OPEC decided not to cut production despite oversupply in the market.
OPEC and partner countries agreed to reduce oil production.

Event_Name: U.S. Withdraws from Iran Nuclear Deal
Description: Reimposed sanctions on Iran reduced expected global oil supply.
Global lockdowns sharply reduced transportation and industrial fuel demand.
OPEC+ agreed to record production cuts to stabilize the oil market.
The conflict disrupted global energy markets and increased supply uncertainty.
